title: Visualizing Total Boundedness in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing a set covered by finitely many epsilon-balls, illustrating the definition of a totally bounded set.
elements: A set S (depicted as a blob)
Multiple points x1, x2, ..., xn in S
Epsilon-balls B(x1, ε), B(x2, ε), ..., B(xn, ε) centered at these points
relationships: The union of all epsilon-balls B(xi, ε) completely covers the set S
The number of balls n is finite for any given ε > 0
